vulnerabilities.description: Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header is missing. CSP helps prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by specifying which content sources are allowed.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add a Content-Security-Policy header. Example: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'

vulnerabilities.description: X-Content-Type-Options header is missing. Without 'nosniff', browsers may MIME-sniff responses, potentially executing malicious content.
HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) header is missing. HSTS ensures browsers always connect via HTTPS, preventing downgrade attacks.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add Strict-Transport-Security header. Example: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains

vulnerabilities.description: Referrer-Policy header is missing. This header controls how much referrer information is sent with requests, protecting user privacy.
Permissions-Policy header is missing. This header controls which browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation) the page can use.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add Permissions-Policy header. Example: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

vulnerabilities.description: Server header exposes server software: 'gws'. Attackers can use this to find known vulnerabilities.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove or obfuscate the Server header in your web server configuration.
Ensure only necessary ports are open. Review firewall rules.
Ensure only necessary ports are open. Review firewall rules.

vulnerabilities.description: Path '/robots.txt' is publicly accessible (HTTP 200). This may expose sensitive data or admin interfaces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Restrict access to '/robots.txt' using authentication, firewall rules, or server configuration. Remove unnecessary files.

vulnerabilities.description: Path '/sitemap.xml' is publicly accessible (HTTP 200). This may expose sensitive data or admin interfaces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Restrict access to '/sitemap.xml' using authentication, firewall rules, or server configuration. Remove unnecessary files.

vulnerabilities.description: Path '/crossdomain.xml' is publicly accessible (HTTP 200). This may expose sensitive data or admin interfaces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Restrict access to '/crossdomain.xml' using authentication, firewall rules, or server configuration. Remove unnecessary files.

vulnerabilities.description: Found 14 subdomains: www.google.com, mail.google.com, admin.google.com, blog.google.com, api.google.com, vpn.google.com, ns1.google.com, ns2.google.com, docs.google.com, support.google.com, shop.google.com, store.google.com, m.google.com, mobile.google.com. Each subdomain increases the attack surface.
vulnerabilities.fix: Audit all subdomains. Ensure unused subdomains are removed and all active ones are secured.
